texto: Technology has made music accessible in a philosophically interesting way, which is great. But on the other hand, when everybody has the ability to make magic, it's like there's no more magic - if the audience can just do it themselves, why are they going to bother?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas Bangalter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Technology has made music accessible in a philosophically interesting way, which is great. But on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, music, great pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de great e magic.

texto: It's very strange how electronic music formatted itself and forgot that its roots are about the surprise, freedom, and the acceptance of every race, gender, and style of music into this big party. Instead, it started to become this electronic lifestyle which also involved the glorification of technology.

texto: Computers were never designed in the first place to become musical instruments. Within a computer, everything is sterile - there's no sound, there's no air. It's totally code. Like with computer-generated effects in movies, you can create wonders. But it's really hard to create emotion.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas Bangalter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. Within a computer…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, movies, emotion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de emotion e you.

texto: Synths are a very low level of artificial intelligence. Whereas you have a Stradivarius that will live for a thousand years.

texto: The concept of the robot encapsulates both aspects of technology. On one hand it's cool, it's fun, it's healthy, it's sexy, it's stylish. On the other hand it's terrifying, it's alienating, it's addictive, and it's scary. That has been the subject of much science-fiction literature.
